mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with contracting industrial output compresses corporate profit margins through higher input costs and weaker volume, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of observations, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a positive coefficient with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer expenditure, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant difference between high and low retail sales growth deciles, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in industrial production below its long-term trend indicates supply-side bottlenecks or weakening manufacturing demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if 60-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of triggered periods, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC consistently exceeds 0.04 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects broad-based economic momentum and business confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month through improved earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth, or the combined signal yields a negative predictive Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates increase corporate borrowing costs and reduce the present value of future cash flows, weighing on equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during tightening phases.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25 percentage points in a single meeting
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 58% of tightening episodes, or forward 60-day returns show no significant difference between hike and hold periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly drives corporate earnings revisions, with positive growth accelerations mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The directional accuracy of the signal drops below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or the correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day equity returns is statistically indistinguishable from zero.
